intro: Macrocarpaea glabra, also known as the "glabrous Macrocarpaea," is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Macrocarpaea glabra include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
